user_message: Summarize what happened with Parnell Aerospace and give me the asks.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Christopher Martinez <christopher.martinez@parnellaeros.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-06 17:59 EST
Subject: URGENT: renewal risk Issue at Parnell Aerospace

Just checking in on the renewal risk proposal. Our VP Operations is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
To: Christopher Martinez <christopher.martinez@parnellaeros.com>
Date: 2024-02-17 16:03 EST
Subject: Re: renewal risk Discussion - Next Steps

There's been a change in our timeline for renewal risk. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

---
From: Christopher Martinez <christopher.martinez@parnellaeros.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-27 12:20 EST
Subject: Follow-up: renewal risk Review

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with renewal risk. What are the next steps from your side?

Meeting: renewal risk Discussion - Parnell Aerospace
Date: 2024-01-18
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Christopher Martinez (Parnell Aerospace), Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft)

[00:02:00] Christopher Martinez (Parnell Aerospace): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on renewal risk from our last session.

[00:05:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): Our CTO reviewed this and has concerns about technical validation. Can we address those?

[00:08:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is budget approval.

[00:10:00] Christopher Martinez (Parnell Aerospace): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is cost optimization.

[00:15:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): The timeline for vendor dependencies is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:19:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is budget approval.

usability_anchors: Score 5: Complete, well-structured output matching the Pre-Meeting Brief template with all required artifacts present and actionable
Score 3: Output covers main requirements but missing one or two secondary artifacts or lacking specificity
Score 1: Output is generic, missing key artifacts, or fails to address the specific request
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct renewal risk decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
